notes: Windows + Cursor CLI (agent) + Ralph method (no upstream bash script). One iteration handles one story.

userStories.title: Add full regression coverage, telemetry updates, and docs for page split refactor
userStories.description: Deliver complete quality, observability, and documentation updates for the RSS Subscriptions and standalone Article Favorites page split.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend/component tests cover entry order, route split, and RSS page removal of embedded favorites block.
E2E scenarios verify navigation and main user flows on RSS订阅 and 文章收藏 as independent pages.
Telemetry/analytics distinguishes entry-click/page-view/core-action events between RSS订阅 and 文章收藏.
Impacted docs are updated with new IA, route changes, and explicit no-compatibility decision.

userStories.title: Simplify add button label on RSS subscriptions page
userStories.description: Remove the redundant 'RSS 订阅' wording from the add button on the RSS subscriptions page so the primary action label is concise and consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: On the RSS 订阅 page, the add button label no longer includes 'RSS 订阅' and displays only '添加'.
Existing behavior for this button remains unchanged, including click action, disabled state, validation, and submit flow.
Related frontend tests are updated and pass without introducing text regressions on other pages.

userStories.title: Normalize add and create labels on article favorites page
userStories.description: Unify action labels on the article favorites page by removing redundant wording and renaming the create action to match the simplified add pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: On the 文章收藏 page, the add button label no longer includes '收藏夹' and displays only '添加'.
The '新建收藏夹' button is renamed to '添加'.
Only text labels are changed; all existing button behaviors remain unchanged.
No conflicting or ambiguous action labels remain on the page after the update.

userStories.title: Align favorites article list controls, item layout, and back navigation
userStories.description: Update the article list under 文章收藏 to match the RSS subscriptions article list behavior for toolbar order, item column order, delete confirmation, and back navigation target.
acceptanceCriteria: In the 文章收藏 article list toolbar, the current '添加文章' button is moved to the left of '刷新' and renamed to '添加'.
Article list row order is aligned with RSS 订阅: star (favorite), date, title, and delete action.
The delete button is right-aligned and requires a confirmation step before deletion proceeds.
The back button from this page navigates to 文章收藏 instead of RSS 订阅.
Frontend tests are updated and pass for toolbar order, row layout order, delete confirmation flow, and back navigation target.

userStories.title: Update RSS subscriptions page heading and add button copy
userStories.description: On the RSS subscriptions page, remove the top-level 'RSS 订阅' heading text and rename the primary add button from '添加' to '添加 Feed' without changing behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: On the RSS 订阅 page, the top heading text 'RSS 订阅' is removed from the page header area.
The primary action button text is renamed from '添加' to '添加 Feed'.
The add flow behavior remains unchanged, including form toggle, validation, submit, and error handling.
Related frontend tests are updated to assert the new copy and pass.

userStories.title: Update article favorites page heading and add button copy
userStories.description: On the article favorites page, remove the top-level '收藏夹' text block and rename the primary add button from '添加' to '添加收藏夹' while preserving existing interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: On the 文章收藏 page, the section heading text '收藏夹' is removed.
The primary action button text is renamed from '添加' to '添加收藏夹'.
Create favorites flow behavior remains unchanged, including modal open/close, validation, submit, and error feedback.
Related frontend tests are updated to assert the new copy and pass.

userStories.title: Align favorites article list star logic with RSS article list
userStories.description: In the article list entered from 文章收藏, replace the static left-side star placeholder with the same interactive favorite-toggle logic used in RSS article lists.
acceptanceCriteria: For virtual/favorites feed article rows, the left star is an interactive favorite-toggle control rather than a static placeholder.
Star icon state is driven by article favorite state, using the same filled/empty rule as RSS article list.
Clicking the control triggers the same favorite API interaction pattern as RSS flow and refreshes list state after success.
Accessibility semantics (aria-label/title) follow the same 收藏/取消收藏 pattern as RSS article list.
Related frontend tests are updated and pass for star rendering and toggle interaction in favorites article list.

userStories.title: Align favorites article list date and age-color block logic with RSS
userStories.description: In the article list entered from 文章收藏, align the left-side date text and age-based color block behavior with the exact implementation used by RSS article list.
acceptanceCriteria: For favorites article rows, date text uses the same year-month display format as RSS article list.
Date wrapper uses the same bordered color-block style pattern as RSS article list.
Age-based color intensity follows the same recency buckets as RSS logic (same-day, <=30 days, <=90 days, >90 days).
Implementation reuses the same date formatting and style decision logic as RSS path rather than introducing divergent rules.
Related frontend tests are updated and pass for date text rendering and age-based class behavior.

userStories.title: Remove '摘要配置' label above summary profile selector in both summary entry paths
userStories.description: In article summary pages reached from both RSS subscriptions and article favorites entry paths, remove the '摘要配置' text shown above the summary-profile dropdown.
acceptanceCriteria: When entering article summary from RSS subscriptions path, the text '摘要配置' above the dropdown is removed.
When entering article summary from article favorites path, the text '摘要配置' above the dropdown is removed.
Summary profile selector remains visible and fully functional in both paths.
No other summary page labels or controls are unintentionally changed.
Related frontend tests are updated and pass for both entry paths.
